zh.lifeSummary: 當Avellina Fitz Alan Mac Gilbert於1198年出生在英國蘇格蘭艾斯爾吉爾馬爾諾可時，她的父親Simon FitzAlan33歲，母親Eschyna33歲。她和Sir Robert Dictus Boyd  ll 1st Laird of Kilmarnock至少有2個兒子。她去世於1263年在蘇格蘭艾斯爾坎寧安享年65歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Avellina Fitz Alan Mac Gilbert于1198年出生在英国苏格兰艾斯爾吉爾馬爾諾可时，她的父亲Simon FitzAlan33岁，母亲Eschyna33岁。她和Sir Robert Dictus Boyd  ll 1st Laird of Kilmarnock至少有2个儿子。她去世于1263年在苏格兰艾斯爾坎寧安享年65岁。

nameMeanings.name: Mac
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Cuong, Minh, Lien, Muoi, Thanh, Tho, Tieu, Vinh, Anh, Beo, Binh, Chau.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 麥 (see   Mai   2) and 莫 (see   Mo   1).
Vietnamese (Mạc): from Chinese 莫 ‘nothing; nonexistent’; see Mo 1.

variants.name: Mai
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Thanh, Hoa, Son, Dung, Long, Binh, Minh, Thu, Hue, Huong, Thuy.  Chinese Chi, Hong, Ho, Sang, Han, Hang, Jian, Li, Man, Mei, Sen, Wei.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 麥, meaning ‘wheat’ in Chinese: (i) from Mai Qiu (麥丘), the title of a man in the state of Qi (located mainly in present-day Shandong province) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), who, despite being very old, came to celebrate the birthday of the Duke Huan of Qi (ruler of the state of Qi, died 643  BC ). His descendants inherited 麥, the first part of his title, as their surname. (ii) borne by descendants of Mai Tie Zhang (麥鐵杖), a famous general during the Sui dynasty (581–618  AD ). His descendants were scattered around southern China.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 買, meaning ‘buy’ in Chinese: (i) borne by descendants of the king Song Weizi, founder of the state of Song. (ii) borne by descendants of the Duke Dao of Xu (ruler of the state of Xu, 546–523  BC ), possibly inheriting his personal name. (iii) from the first element of the personal name Mai Zhu Chu (買朱鉏), style name of an official who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iv) it has also been traced back to other ethnic groups in northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Gilbert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin), French, and German: from the personal name Giselbert, composed of the ancient Germanic elements gīsil ‘pledge, hostage, noble youth’ (see Giesel ) + berht ‘bright, famous’. This personal name enjoyed considerable popularity in England during the Middle Ages, partly as a result of the fame of Saint Gilbert of Sempringham (1085–1189), the founder of the only native English monastic order.
Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish surnames.

History: The Devon family of Gilbert can be traced to Geoffrey Gilbert (died 1349), who represented Totnes in Parliament in 1326. His descendants included Sir Humphrey Gilbert (died 1583), who discovered Newfoundland. — Étienne Gilbert from Aulnay in Vienne, France, married Marguerite Thibault in Neuville, QC, in 1683. Louis Gilbert dit Comtois from Besançon in Doubs married Anne Jacques in Charlesbourg, QC, in 1722. Pierre Gilbert from Barbezieux-Saint-Hilaire in Charente, France, married Marie-Angélique Dufour in Petite-Rivière-Saint-François, QC, in 1756. Charles Dupuis dit Gilbert from Rosnay in Indre, France, married Marie-Jeanne Brunet in Sainte-Foy, QC, in 1741.

variants.name: Gieseke
meanings: North German: from a pet form of the personal name Giselbert (see Gilbert ). Compare Giesecke .

variants.name: Giliberto
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Sal, Salvatore, Constantino, Costantino, Emilio, Rosario, Santo, Sebastiano, Vittorio.
Italian: from an ancient Germanic personal name (see   Gilbert  ).

variants.name: Gipp
meanings: English (East Anglia): from the personal name Gipp, a pet form of Gilbert . See also Gibbs . Alternatively, if pronounced with a soft g, it is a variant of Jepp, from the Middle English personal name Jep(p), Gep(pe), a pet form of Jeffrey .
German: from a short form of an ancient Germanic personal name formed with the stem geb ‘gift’, as in Gebhard (see Gebhardt ).

variants.name: Gilbertson
meanings: English (northern): from the personal name Gilbert + -son.

variants.name: Giesel
meanings: German: from the personal name Giesel, a short form of any of several ancient Germanic personal names with the first element gīsil ‘pledge, hostage, noble offspring’. In ancient and medieval ancient Germanic society, the younger children of kings and princes were sometimes sent to be brought up at the court of a neighboring ruler, as a pledge of peace between the two nations or clans. Thus, the male personal name Gisil and its feminine equivalent Gisela mean both ‘pledge’ and ‘noble offspring’.

variants.name: Gilb
meanings: German: from a short form of the personal name   Gilbert  .

variants.name: Gibert
meanings: French and German: from a shortened form of the personal name   Gilbert  . This name was also brought to England by Huguenot refugees in the 17th century.
History: This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of South Carolina.

variants.name: Giese
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Klaus, Erwin, Heiner, Heinz, Uwe, Aloysius, Armin, Dieter, Erhard, Ewald, Frieda.

German, Dutch, and Danish: from a short form of the personal name Giselbert (see Gilbert ), or any other ancient Germanic name with gīsil ‘hostage, pledge, noble offspring’ (see Giesel ) as the first element. Compare Giesey .

variants.name: Gibby
meanings: English (Pembrokeshire): from a Middle English pet form of the personal name   Gilbert  .

variants.name: Gebbie
meanings: Scottish (Ayrshire and Lanarkshire): from a pet form of the Norman personal name Giselbert (see Gilbert ).

nameMeanings.name: Avellina
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the Norman name   Evelyn .

nameMeanings.name: Fitz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Either a short form of any of the numerous names representing a transferred use of a surname formed with the Anglo-Norman French prefix fi(t)z ‘son of ’, as for example Fitzgerald , Fitzroy , or a transferred use of the surname Fitz, which may be of the same derivation, but applied originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same given name, or a local name from Fitz in Shropshire, named with the Old English personal name Fitt + hōh ‘hill spur’.

nameMeanings.name: Alan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

en.lifeSummary: When Avellina Fitz Alan Mac Gilbert was born in 1198, in Kilmarnock, Ayrshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, her father, Simon FitzAlan, was 33 and her mother, Eschyna, was 33. She had at least 2 sons with Sir Robert Dictus Boyd  ll 1st Laird of Kilmarnock. She died in 1263, in Cunningham, Ayrshire, Scotland, at the age of 65.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Avellina Fitz Alan Mac Gilbert est née en 1198, à Kilmarnock, Ayrshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Simon FitzAlan, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Eschyna, avait 33 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils avec Sir Robert Dictus Boyd  ll 1st Laird of Kilmarnock. Elle est décédée en 1263, à Cunningham, Ayrshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 65 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Avellina Fitz Alan Mac Gilbert 1198 in Kilmarnock, Ayrshire, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Simon FitzAlan 33 und ihre Mutter Eschyna 33. Sie hatte mit Sir Robert Dictus Boyd  ll 1st Laird of Kilmarnock mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb 1263 im Alter von 65 Jahren in Cunningham, Ayrshire, Scotland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Avellina Fitz Alan Mac Gilbert è nata il nel 1198 nel Kilmarnock, Ayrshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, sua padre, Simon FitzAlan, aveva 33 anni e sua madre, Eschyna, aveva 33 anni. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli con Sir Robert Dictus Boyd  ll 1st Laird of Kilmarnock. Morì nel 1263, nel Cunningham, Ayrshire, Scotland, all'età di 65 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Avellina Fitz Alan Mac Gilbert(이)가 1198년 영국 스코틀랜드 에이르실르 킬마노c크에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Simon FitzAlan(은)는 33세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Eschyna(은)는 33세였다. 그녀는 Sir Robert Dictus Boyd  ll 1st Laird of Kilmarnock(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1263년 스컷랜드 에이르실르 쿤잉함에서 65세의 나이로 사망했다.
